description: Microsoft Outlook Remote Code Execution Vulnerability. An attacker who successfully exploited this vulnerability could execute arbitrary code in the context of the current user. If the current user is logged on with administrative user rights, an attacker could take control of the affected system.
cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code

description: XZ Utils backdoor vulnerability. Malicious code was discovered in the upstream tarballs of xz, starting with version 5.6.0. The liblzma build process extracts a prebuilt object file which modifies specific functions to intercept and modify data interaction with the library.
vendor_advisory: Revert to XZ Utils 5.4.6 or earlier

description: HTTP/2 Rapid Reset Attack. The HTTP/2 protocol allows a denial of service because request cancellation can reset many streams quickly. The attacker sends a stream of requests over a single HTTP/2 connection, immediately canceling each one.

Fortinet FortiOS and FortiProxy Out-of-bounds Write Vulnerability. An out-of-bounds write vulnerability in FortiOS and FortiProxy SSL-VPN may allow a remote unauthenticated attacker to execute arbitrary code or commands via specifically crafted requests.

Jenkins Arbitrary File Read Vulnerability. A vulnerability in the CLI allows attackers to read arbitrary files on the Jenkins controller file system by exploiting how the CLI processes file path arguments.
cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname

description: Apache ActiveMQ Remote Code Execution. The Java OpenWire protocol marshaller is vulnerable to Remote Code Execution. This vulnerability may allow a remote attacker with network access to a broker to run arbitrary shell commands.

JetBrains TeamCity Authentication Bypass Vulnerability. An authentication bypass vulnerability allows an unauthenticated attacker with HTTP(S) access to gain administrative control of that TeamCity server.
Linux Kernel Use-After-Free Vulnerability. A use-after-free flaw in the Linux kernel's netfilter subsystem in nf_tables can be exploited by an unprivileged local user to gain root privileges.
libwebp Heap Buffer Overflow. A heap buffer overflow in libwebp allows a remote attacker to perform an out of bounds memory write via a crafted WebP image file.

Fortinet FortiOS SQL Injection Vulnerability. A SQL injection vulnerability in Fortinet FortiOS and FortiProxy allows a privileged attacker to execute unauthorized code or commands via specially crafted requests.
curl SOCKS5 Heap Buffer Overflow. A heap buffer overflow vulnerability in curl's SOCKS5 proxy handshake allows attackers to execute arbitrary code via a malicious SOCKS5 proxy server.
PHP CGI Argument Injection Vulnerability. An argument injection vulnerability in PHP-CGI on Windows allows remote code execution via specially crafted query strings when PHP is used in CGI mode.
OpenSSH regreSSHion Remote Code Execution. A signal handler race condition in OpenSSH's server allows unauthenticated remote code execution as root on glibc-based Linux systems.

Atlassian Confluence Data Center Privilege Escalation. A broken access control vulnerability allows an unauthenticated attacker to create unauthorized Confluence administrator accounts and access Confluence instances.

Fortinet FortiOS Heap Buffer Overflow. A heap-based buffer overflow vulnerability in FortiOS SSL-VPN may allow a remote unauthenticated attacker to execute arbitrary code via specifically crafted requests.
Cisco ASA and FTD Software Web Services Denial of Service. A vulnerability in the web services interface could allow an unauthenticated remote attacker to cause a denial of service condition.
Cisco IOS XE Web UI Privilege Escalation. A vulnerability in the web UI feature allows a remote unauthenticated attacker to create an account with privilege level 15 access.
affected_products: Cisco IOS XE Software with Web UI enabled

description: Ivanti Connect Secure Command Injection. A command injection vulnerability in Ivanti Connect Secure allows an authenticated administrator to send specially crafted requests and execute arbitrary commands.
Ivanti Connect Secure Privilege Escalation. A privilege escalation vulnerability allows a user to elevate privileges to that of an administrator.
Fortinet FortiClient EMS SQL Injection. A SQL injection vulnerability in FortiClient EMS allows an unauthenticated attacker to execute unauthorized code or commands via specifically crafted requests.
Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS Command Injection. A command injection vulnerability in the GlobalProtect feature allows an unauthenticated attacker to execute arbitrary code with root privileges.

Microsoft Outlook Elevation of Privilege. An elevation of privilege vulnerability exists when Microsoft Outlook fails to properly handle objects in memory. An attacker can send a specially crafted email to automatically send Net-NTLMv2 hashes.
Microsoft Office Remote Code Execution. A remote code execution vulnerability exists in Microsoft Office where an attacker could execute arbitrary code via a specially crafted document.
Windows Error Reporting Service Elevation of Privilege. An elevation of privilege vulnerability in Windows Error Reporting Service allows an attacker to gain SYSTEM privileges.
Microsoft SmartScreen Prompt Security Feature Bypass. A security feature bypass vulnerability allows an attacker to bypass SmartScreen user prompts for malicious files.
Ivanti Endpoint Manager Mobile Authentication Bypass. An authentication bypass vulnerability allows remote unauthenticated attackers to access restricted functionality or resources.
Ivanti Endpoint Manager Mobile Path Traversal. A path traversal vulnerability allows an unauthenticated attacker to access restricted functionality or resources without authentication.
WinRAR Code Execution Vulnerability. A vulnerability in WinRAR allows attackers to execute arbitrary code when a user attempts to view a benign file within a ZIP archive.
Citrix ADC and Gateway Code Injection. A code injection vulnerability allows an unauthenticated attacker to perform remote code execution on appliances configured as a Gateway or AAA virtual server.
Microsoft Exchange Server Spoofing Vulnerability. A spoofing vulnerability allows an attacker to perform spoofing attacks via specially crafted requests.
ownCloud Graphapi Information Disclosure. An information disclosure vulnerability in ownCloud graphapi allows attackers to access sensitive information including admin passwords.
JetBrains TeamCity Authentication Bypass. An authentication bypass vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to gain administrative access to TeamCity servers.

Fortinet FortiOS Format String Vulnerability. A format string vulnerability in FortiOS fgfmd daemon may allow a remote unauthenticated attacker to execute arbitrary code via specially crafted requests.
Apache Struts File Upload Path Traversal. A path traversal vulnerability in file upload logic allows attackers to perform remote code execution via manipulated file upload parameters.
Cisco ASA and FTD VPN Denial of Service. A vulnerability in the remote access VPN feature allows an unauthenticated remote attacker to cause a denial of service condition.
Apache RocketMQ Remote Code Execution. A remote code execution vulnerability allows attackers to execute arbitrary code by exploiting the way Apache RocketMQ handles certain requests.

Atlassian Confluence Improper Authorization. An improper authorization vulnerability in Atlassian Confluence Data Center and Server allows an unauthenticated attacker to reset Confluence and create unauthorized administrator accounts.
Apache OFBiz Path Traversal. A path traversal vulnerability in Apache OFBiz allows attackers to execute arbitrary code via specially crafted URL parameters.
Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS Management Interface Authentication Bypass. An authentication bypass vulnerability in the management web interface allows unauthenticated attackers to gain admin privileges.
ConnectWise ScreenConnect Path Traversal. A path traversal vulnerability in ScreenConnect allows attackers to execute arbitrary code or access sensitive files.
ConnectWise ScreenConnect Authentication Bypass. An authentication bypass vulnerability allows attackers to gain unauthorized access to ScreenConnect instances.
F5 BIG-IP Configuration Utility SQL Injection. A SQL injection vulnerability in the BIG-IP Configuration utility allows an authenticated attacker to execute arbitrary SQL commands.
F5 BIG-IP Configuration Utility Authentication Bypass. An authentication bypass vulnerability allows an unauthenticated attacker with network access to perform administrative operations.
Cisco ASA and FTD Remote Code Execution. A remote code execution vulnerability in the web services interface allows an authenticated remote attacker to execute arbitrary code.
Cisco IOS XE Local Privilege Escalation. A local privilege escalation vulnerability allows an authenticated local attacker to execute commands with root privileges.
Progress MOVEit Transfer SQL Injection. A SQL injection vulnerability in MOVEit Transfer allows unauthenticated attackers to gain unauthorized access to the database.
Progress MOVEit Transfer SQL Injection. A SQL injection vulnerability found in the MOVEit Transfer web application allows unauthenticated attackers to gain unauthorized access.
Progress MOVEit Transfer SQL Injection. Multiple SQL injection vulnerabilities found in the MOVEit Transfer web application could allow an authenticated attacker to gain unauthorized access.
Windows DWM Core Library Elevation of Privilege. An elevation of privilege vulnerability allows an attacker to gain SYSTEM privileges.
Windows Telephony Service Elevation of Privilege. An elevation of privilege vulnerability exists in the Windows Telephony Service that allows attackers to gain SYSTEM privileges.
Microsoft Exchange Server Elevation of Privilege. An elevation of privilege vulnerability allows an attacker to perform NTLM relay attacks against Exchange Server.
Ivanti Endpoint Manager SQL Injection. Multiple SQL injection vulnerabilities in Ivanti Endpoint Manager allow remote authenticated attackers to execute arbitrary SQL commands.
Ivanti Connect Secure XXE Vulnerability. An XML external entity (XXE) vulnerability in Ivanti Connect Secure allows authenticated attackers to read arbitrary files.
VMware vCenter Server Heap Overflow. A heap overflow vulnerability in VMware vCenter Server allows a malicious actor with network access to potentially execute remote code.

VMware Aria Operations Deserialization Vulnerability. An unauthenticated attacker with network access to VMware Aria Operations can execute arbitrary code due to an unsafe deserialization vulnerability.

VMware vCenter Server Out-of-Bounds Write. An out-of-bounds write vulnerability in the implementation of the DCERPC protocol allows a malicious actor to trigger an out-of-bounds write potentially leading to remote code execution.
VMware vCenter Server Privilege Escalation. A local privilege escalation vulnerability allows an authenticated user to escalate privileges on vCenter Server appliance.
Cisco IOS XE Web UI Privilege Escalation (second instance). A privilege escalation vulnerability in the web UI feature allows an authenticated attacker to elevate privileges to root.
Cisco Smart Licensing Utility Static Credential Vulnerability. A vulnerability in Cisco Smart Licensing Utility allows an attacker to access the system using static credentials.
Cisco IOS XE Software Web UI Privilege Escalation. A privilege escalation vulnerability in the web UI allows an authenticated remote attacker to elevate privileges to Administrator.
OpenSSH pre-authentication Double Free. A double-free vulnerability in OpenSSH allows unauthenticated attackers to cause a denial of service or potentially execute arbitrary code.
VMware ESXi Authentication Bypass. An authentication bypass vulnerability in ESXi allows a malicious actor with sufficient Active Directory permissions to gain full access to an ESXi host.
VMware vCenter Server SSRF Vulnerability. A server-side request forgery (SSRF) vulnerability in vCenter Server allows an authenticated attacker to send requests to arbitrary systems.
WordPress LiteSpeed Cache Plugin RCE. A remote code execution vulnerability in the LiteSpeed Cache plugin for WordPress allows unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary code.
Cacti Command Injection. A command injection vulnerability in Cacti allows authenticated users to execute arbitrary commands on the underlying server.
GeoServer GeoTools JNDI Injection. A JNDI injection vulnerability in GeoServer's GeoTools library allows remote code execution through specially crafted requests.
Rejetto HTTP File Server RCE. A template injection vulnerability in Rejetto HTTP File Server allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands via specially crafted HTTP requests.
Apache OFBiz Path Traversal and RCE. A path traversal vulnerability combined with insufficient validation allows attackers to achieve remote code execution.
GeoServer OGC Filter SQL Injection. A SQL injection vulnerability in the OGC Filter evaluator allows remote code execution through GeoTools property functions.
Ivanti Connect Secure Authentication Bypass. An authentication bypass vulnerability allows remote attackers to access restricted resources without authentication.

Ivanti Connect Secure Server-Side Request Forgery. A server-side request forgery vulnerability allows authenticated attackers to send web requests to arbitrary systems through the Ivanti Connect Secure appliance.
Apache OFBiz Cross-Site Scripting to RCE. A cross-site scripting vulnerability that can be escalated to achieve server-side template injection and remote code execution.
Jenkins Remote Code Execution. A remote code execution vulnerability in Jenkins allows attackers to execute arbitrary commands via specially crafted requests.
GitLab Pipeline Execution as Another User. A vulnerability allows malicious actors to execute pipelines as another user under specific conditions.
Android Privilege Escalation Vulnerability. A vulnerability in Android allows local attackers to escalate privileges and gain unauthorized access to system resources.
